title: --top-module does not work if module name has double underscores
body:
I am using Verilator version 5.044. I think there might be an issue with --top-module if the module name has a double underscore. This works fine:

```
% cat > top_module.v \
<<'END'
 module top_module;
   initial begin
     $display("Hello World");
     $finish;
   end
 endmodule
END

% verilator --binary --top-module top_module top_module.v
% ./obj_dir/Vtop_module
Hello World
- top_module.v:4: Verilog $finish
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.044 2026-01-01
- Verilator: $finish at 0s; walltime 0.000 s; speed 0.000 s/s
- Verilator: cpu 0.000 s on 1 threads; alloced 16 MB
```

Then I tried replacing `top_module` with `top__module` like this:

```
% cat > top__module.v \
<<'END'
 module top__module;
   initial begin
     $display("Hello World");
     $finish;
   end
 endmodule
END

% verilator --binary --top-module top__module top__module.v
%Error: Specified --top-module 'top__module' was not found in design.
       ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.044 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

This used to work with Verilator version 5.032.
code_blocks:
% cat > top_module.v \
<<'END'
 module top_module;
   initial begin
     $display("Hello World");
     $finish;
   end
 endmodule
END

% verilator --binary --top-module top_module top_module.v
% ./obj_dir/Vtop_module
Hello World
- top_module.v:4: Verilog $finish
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.044 2026-01-01
- Verilator: $finish at 0s; walltime 0.000 s; speed 0.000 s/s
- Verilator: cpu 0.000 s on 1 threads; alloced 16 MB
% cat > top__module.v \
<<'END'
 module top__module;
   initial begin
     $display("Hello World");
     $finish;
   end
 endmodule
END

% verilator --binary --top-module top__module top__module.v
%Error: Specified --top-module 'top__module' was not found in design.
       ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html?v=5.044 for more assistance.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
